description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple large, rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a structured base. The panels are arranged in two distinct rows, each row slightly elevated and tilted, likely to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be a solid platform with a patterned surface, possibly simulating concrete or metal, and includes several raised and recessed sections that may represent electrical housings or access points. The solar panels themselves have a grid-like texture, indicative of individual solar cells, and are supported by visible mounting frames. The overall structure is expansive and low-profile, suggesting a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent from the exterior views.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar array with multiple tilted photovoltaic panels on a patterned, solid base. The structure features two rows of panels and several raised sections on the platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing two rows of tilted solar panels on a wide, patterned base with several raised sections.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat expanse of the base and the backs of the solar panels, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the alignment and tilt of the panels, as well as the distribution of raised sections on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible support frames, likely aluminum or steel structure
attribute_array_configuration: two parallel rows of solar panels, each row containing approximately 4-5 panels in a linear arrangement
